daily_brief: The US-Iran conflict dominated global news on Monday as new US-Israeli strikes killed more than 25 people across Iran, including at least six children, while President Trump issued an expletive-laden ultimatum demanding Tehran reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday or face attacks on civilian infrastructure [France 24, Globe and Mail, Al Jazeera]. The weekend saw a dramatic US military rescue of a downed F-15 crew member from Iranian territory, narrowly averting a potential hostage crisis [BBC, Globe and Mail]. Amid the conflict, Pope Leo XIV delivered his first Easter Mass, calling on world leaders to 'choose peace' without naming specific countries [BBC, RFI]. In Europe, Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán convened emergency security talks after explosives were found near a Russian gas pipeline in Serbia, with opposition figures warning of a possible false flag operation ahead of Sunday's elections [BBC]. Meanwhile, Artemis II astronauts prepared for humanity's first lunar flyby since 1972 [France 24, The Hindu], and Ukraine continued expanding its regional security partnerships with President Zelensky visiting Damascus to discuss military cooperation with Syria's new leadership [RFI, France 24].

top_stories.headline: US-Israeli Strikes Kill Over 25 in Iran as Trump Issues Tuesday Ultimatum on Strait of Hormuz
bullets: New wave of US-Israeli airstrikes overnight killed at least 25 people in Iran, including six children, with targets including a residential area in Tehran and Sharif University of Technology
President Trump posted expletive-filled threat demanding Iran reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday 8 PM ET or face attacks on power plants and bridges
Pakistan has proposed a two-tier ceasefire framework to both Iran and the US that could take effect Monday and reopen the strait
Iran's military command warned of 'much more devastating' retaliation if civilian targets continue to be hit, while dismissing Trump as an 'unstable, delusional figure'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Led with casualty figures and civilian impact, noted Pakistan mediation effort

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on Trump's rhetoric and shifting deadlines, quoted Iranian culture minister

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Emphasized civilian deaths and Iran's rejection of ultimatum, included regional economic impacts

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage focusing on oil price implications

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Detailed strike locations and Iranian response, emphasized civilian infrastructure targeting

top_stories.headline: US Military Rescues Downed F-15 Crew Member from Iranian Territory in Complex Operation
bullets: US special forces extracted a wounded weapons systems officer who had evaded capture for over 24 hours in mountainous Iranian terrain after his F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down Friday
CIA conducted deception campaign inside Iran to misdirect search efforts while rescue was underway; Iran had offered $66,000 bounty for the airman's capture
Two Black Hawk helicopters came under fire and two transport planes had to be destroyed during the operation
First US fighter jet shot down by enemy fire in over 20 years; incident highlights continued Iranian air defense capabilities despite weeks of strikes

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Detailed operational account, noted this challenges Trump's claims of 'overwhelming air dominance'

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Historical context comparing to Cold War-era pilot captures, emphasized avoided hostage crisis

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Straightforward factual coverage of rescue details

top_stories.headline: Hungary's Orbán Convenes Emergency Council After Explosives Found Near Russian Gas Pipeline
bullets: Two rucksacks of explosives and detonators found by Serbian army approximately 20km from where TurkStream pipeline crosses into Hungary
Discovery comes one week before Hungarian elections where Orbán's Fidesz party trails significantly in polls
Opposition leader Péter Magyar accused government of 'panic-mongering' orchestrated by 'Russian advisers'; security experts had previously warned of possible false flag operation
Serbian President Vučić, a close Orbán ally, personally informed Hungarian leader of the discovery

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provided context on prior false flag warnings and election implications

top_stories.headline: Pope Leo XIV Delivers First Easter Address, Calls for Peace Without Naming Conflicts
bullets: First US-born pope addressed thousands in St Peter's Square, calling on 'those who have the power to unleash wars' to choose peace
Broke with recent papal tradition by not explicitly naming any country or conflict in his message
Announced prayer vigil at Vatican for April 11; paid tribute to predecessor Pope Francis who died hours after last year's Easter address
Has emerged as vocal critic of Iran war through recent public statements urging de-escalation

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Noted break with tradition of naming conflicts, highlighted Pope's criticism of Iran war

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Middle East war context overshadowing celebrations

top_stories.headline: Artemis II Astronauts Begin Historic Lunar Flyby, First Since 1972
bullets: Orion capsule entered Moon's gravitational sphere of influence Monday; crew will travel farther from Earth than any humans before
Mission includes first woman (Christina Koch), first person of color (Victor Glover), and first non-American (Canadian Jeremy Hansen) to fly around the Moon
Approximately 40 minutes of radio silence expected as spacecraft passes behind Moon
NASA broadcasting live on multiple platforms; flyby lasting roughly seven hours began around 1845 GMT

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on historic firsts and technical details of the mission

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized scientific achievements and documentation of lunar features

additional_stories.headline: Ukrainian forces confirmed operating in western Libya, carried out attack on Russian shadow fleet tanker
Ukraine's Zelensky meets Syria's new leader in Damascus to discuss military cooperation and food security
Nigerian military rescues 31 worshippers abducted during Easter church services in Kaduna state; five killed in attack
Russian drone attack on market in southern Ukraine kills five, injures 21 including child
German males under 45 may now require military approval for stays abroad longer than three months under new defense law
Iraq resumes oil exports via Turkey pipeline as Middle East war reshapes trade routes away from Strait of Hormuz
Vietnam's gig workers face economic hardship as fuel prices double amid Iran's Hormuz blockade
China executes French citizen convicted of drug trafficking after 20 years in prison despite Paris protests
New Zealand Foreign Minister Winston Peters traveling to Washington to meet Secretary of State Rubio amid Middle East tensions
Burkina Faso's military ruler declares democracy 'not for us,' tells citizens to forget about elections
